question: What is agentic RAG and how does it differ from standard RAG?

claim_lines: Agentic RAG is RAG with an agent/controller layer. Instead of a single retrieve-once-then-generate pass, the system can plan, choose tools, retrieve again, and verify or refine its output before answering. Standard RAG is the simpler baseline: retrieve relevant external context, then generate from that context. https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401
- standard retrieve-then-generate workflow — Standard RAG combines a language model with retrieved non-parametric memory for generation: https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401
- planning or iterative retrieval — Agentic RAG uses planning and iterative retrieval/refinement rather than a single static pass: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136

- tool use — Agentic RAG explicitly uses tool use, and framework docs show agents calling tools in long-running loops: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/overview

- reflection or verification — Evaluator-optimizer workflows add a separate evaluation step and loop back when feedback says the answer needs improvement: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/workflows-agents

- added complexity and failure modes — The extra autonomy adds orchestration complexity and open issues around evaluation, coordination, memory management, efficiency, and governance: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136

Direct source-backed summary: the survey describes Agentic RAG as embedding autonomous AI agents into the RAG pipeline, with reflection, planning, tool use, and multi-agent collaboration; the baseline RAG paper describes retrieve-then-generate with a retriever over non-parametric memory. https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401

Engineering inference: agentic RAG is most useful when a query needs multi-step reasoning, source selection, or self-checking; standard RAG is usually enough when one retrieval pass is likely sufficient. That recommendation follows from the cited loop/verification patterns and the added complexity noted in the survey, not from a direct benchmark in the selected sources. https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09136 https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/workflows-agents
